summary: Tractate Yoma daf 88a covers: Havdalah after Yom Kippur: recited in the Havdil blessing of Ma'ariv; dispute about location; Seminal emission (keri) on Yom Kippur: whether immersion is required.

display.whats: Havdalah after Yom Kippur: recited in the Havdil blessing of Ma'ariv; dispute about location
display.hint: The Gemara discusses where Havdalah (the separation prayer marking the end of YK) is recited: in the Havdil blessing within the Amida of Ma'ariv, or separately. Various opinions are cited.

learning.summary: The Gemara discusses where Havdalah (the separation prayer marking the end of YK) is recited: in the Havdil blessing within the Amida of Ma'ariv, or separately. Various opinions are cited.
learning.ahaMoment: Havdalah placement: the end of YK mirrors its opening - the transition out of holiness requires its own formal marking, just as the entry did.
learning.memoryAnchor: Havdalah after YK: the exit from holiness is marked as carefully as the entry.
learning.openQuestion: What is the deeper significance of the ruling about havdalah after yom kippur: recited in the havdil blessing of?

argumentFlow.label: Why havdala forces a full evening Amida
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The reason for the full eighteen-blessing evening Amida after Yom Kippur: one must say havdala in the blessing 'who graciously grants knowledge,' and an abridged Amida has no place to insert it. This shows the tannaim who require the full Amida hold that ne'ila does NOT exempt the evening prayer - turning the dispute into one about immersion timing.

argumentFlow.label: Who immerses on Yom Kippur
argumentFlow.speaker: Baraita
argumentFlow.text: A baraita: anyone needing immersion immerses normally on Yom Kippur, since this is not the forbidden 'washing.' A niddah and a new mother, who immerse at night, immerse on the night of Yom Kippur in the usual way.

argumentFlow.label: Seminal emission and the timing of immersion
argumentFlow.speaker: Baraita
argumentFlow.text: One who had a seminal emission on Yom Kippur before mincha may immerse any time until mincha so he can pray it (per Ezra's decree that a ba'al keri immerse before praying or learning). If it happened after mincha, he waits until nightfall to immerse - showing ne'ila is a nighttime prayer, so it exempts ma'ariv. But Rabbi Yosei says he may immerse any time in the day - holding ne'ila is daytime, so it does NOT exempt ma'ariv.

argumentFlow.label: A contradiction in Rabbi Yosei
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: A second baraita has Rabbi Yosei say a ba'al keri may NOT immerse from mincha onward - the opposite of the first. The Gemara reconciles: the 'may not immerse' case is where he already prayed ne'ila before noticing the emission; the 'may immerse' case is where he had not yet prayed ne'ila and immerses in order to pray it.

argumentFlow.label: Why the Rabbis permit immersion even after ne'ila
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: If he already prayed ne'ila, why do the Rabbis still let him immerse on Yom Kippur, seemingly pointlessly? Because the Rabbis hold immersion at its proper time is itself a mitzva, even without a prayer to enable - so when the time falls on Yom Kippur, he immerses regardless.

argumentFlow.label: Does Rabbi Yosei reject immersion-at-its-time as a mitzva?
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: By inference Rabbi Yosei holds immersion at its proper time is not a mitzva. But a baraita about a divine name written in ink on the flesh seems to show the opposite: one may not wash or smear it lest it be erased, yet if the time for a mitzva-immersion comes he wraps a reed over the name and immerses. The Gemara must square this with Rabbi Yosei's view.

argumentFlow.label: The reed dispute over immersion-at-its-time
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: In that baraita the Rabbis and Rabbi Yosei disagree precisely over tevila bizmanah. The Rabbis say immersion at its proper time is not a mitzva, so without a reed one waits even until the next day rather than risk erasing the name. Rabbi Yosei says it is a mitzva, so one immerses at the proper time even without covering the name - the opposite of the inference drawn above.

argumentFlow.label: Reattributing the baraita to Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The resolution: the 'Rabbi Yosei' of that reed baraita is actually Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda, who taught 'it suffices that the immersion be at the end' - one need only immerse once when surely pure, not repeatedly to hit the exact proper time. So he too holds immersion-at-its-time is not a binding mitzva, removing the contradiction.

argumentFlow.label: Rubbing the skin before immersion
argumentFlow.speaker: Baraita
argumentFlow.text: The Sages taught: one who sees an emission on Yom Kippur immerses, and 'in the evening rubs his skin' to remove interpositions. The Gemara objects: why rub afterward - what's done is done. Rather, he should rub from the eve of Yom Kippur, in case he must immerse during the day. This Master holds rubbing to remove interpositions is itself a mitzva.

requiresUnderstanding: Understanding of Yom Kippur halachic framework

misconceptions.misconception: Whether to immerse on Yom Kippur is just about being clean for prayer.
misconceptions.correction: The deeper dispute is whether immersion at its proper time (tevila bizmanah) is an independent mitzva. The Rabbis say yes, so one immerses even with no prayer to enable; Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda effectively says no.

visualizableElements.name: Havdalah after Yom Kippur: recited in the Havdil b

quizSeeds.q: Why does the need to say havdala require a full evening Amida after Yom Kippur?
quizSeeds.a: Havdala is inserted into the blessing 'who graciously grants knowledge,' and an abridged Amida has no place to put it. So those tannaim require the full eighteen blessings, implying ne'ila does not exempt the evening prayer.

quizSeeds.q: How does the Gemara resolve the two contradictory baraitot about whether Rabbi Yosei lets a ba'al keri immerse after mincha?
quizSeeds.a: The 'may not immerse' case is where he already prayed ne'ila before noticing the emission; the 'may immerse' case is where he had not yet prayed ne'ila and needs to immerse in order to pray it. The timing relative to ne'ila reconciles them.

quizSeeds.q: Why do the Rabbis permit immersion on Yom Kippur even after one has already prayed ne'ila?
quizSeeds.a: Because they hold that immersion at its proper time is itself a mitzva, independent of needing to pray. So when the proper time falls on Yom Kippur, the person immerses regardless of whether an immediate prayer requires it.

display.whats: Seminal emission (keri) on Yom Kippur: whether immersion is required
display.hint: The Gemara discusses whether a man who experiences a seminal emission on Yom Kippur is required to immerse in a mikveh. The discussion touches on the broader question of immersion for keri on Shabbat ...

learning.summary: The Gemara discusses whether a man who experiences a seminal emission on Yom Kippur is required to immerse in a mikveh. The discussion touches on the broader question of immersion for keri on Shabbat and holidays.
learning.ahaMoment: Keri and immersion on YK: the requirement to immerse was eventually abolished for Shabbat and holidays, but the Gemara works through the logic of when it might still apply.
learning.memoryAnchor: Keri on YK: immersion requirements and their relationship to the day's prohibitions.
learning.openQuestion: What is the deeper significance of the ruling about seminal emission (keri) on yom kippur: whether immersion is ?

argumentFlow.label: A seminal emission on Yom Kippur: sins forgiven
argumentFlow.speaker: Baraita / Rav Nachman
argumentFlow.text: A baraita taught before Rav Nachman: one who has a seminal emission on Yom Kippur - his sins are forgiven. The Gemara contrasts another baraita saying his sins are 'arranged before him,' and reconciles: 'arranged' means arranged to be forgiven. The involuntary emission is read as a favorable sign.

argumentFlow.label: The school of Rabbi Yishmael: worry, then reassurance
argumentFlow.speaker: Tanna of R. Yishmael
argumentFlow.text: The school of Rabbi Yishmael: such a person should worry the whole year that perhaps his fast was rejected. But if he survives the year, he is assured his merits protected him and earned him a share in the World to Come. Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak adds: while the whole world is 'hungry' (abstaining from relations on Yom Kippur), he is 'satisfied' - a sign of divine compassion for the involuntary event.

argumentFlow.label: Rav Dimi: a sign of long life
argumentFlow.speaker: Rav Dimi
argumentFlow.text: When Rav Dimi came from Eretz Yisrael he said: a seminal emission on Yom Kippur is a sign one will live long, grow, and raise others, alluding to 'that he might see his seed and prolong his days' (Isaiah 53:10). The chapter closes on this reassuring, redemptive note.

theological: Keri and immersion on YK: the requirement to immerse was eventually abolished fo
narrative: Seminal emission (keri) on Yom Kippur: whether immersion is

requiresUnderstanding: Understanding of Yom Kippur halachic framework

misconceptions.misconception: A seminal emission on Yom Kippur is purely a bad omen of a rejected fast.
misconceptions.correction: Although Rabbi Yishmael's school says one should worry that year, the prevailing reading is favorable: the involuntary emission is a sign of divine compassion, forgiveness, and even long life per Rav Dimi.

visualizableElements.name: Seminal emission (keri) on Yom Kippur: whether imm

quizSeeds.q: How does the Gemara reconcile that one who has an emission on Yom Kippur has 'his sins forgiven' with the source saying his sins are 'arranged before him'?
quizSeeds.a: 'Arranged' (mesudarin) is read as 'arranged to be forgiven.' Both sources agree the involuntary emission is a favorable sign pointing toward forgiveness, not condemnation.

quizSeeds.q: What does Rav Dimi say a seminal emission on Yom Kippur signifies, and what verse alludes to it?
quizSeeds.a: That the person will live long, grow, and raise others - a sign of long life - alluding to 'that he might see his seed and prolong his days' (Isaiah 53:10).

rashiTranslations.he: תנאי היא. אי פטרה נעילה את של ערבית אי לא: הכי גרסינן
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi: Havdalah after Yom Kippur is recited in the Havdil blessing of Ma'ariv.

rashiTranslations.he: בתוספתא כל חייבי עבילות טובלין כדרכן ביום הכפורים הנדה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi: the question of where exactly Havdalah is inserted in the YK Ma'ariv.

rashiTranslations.he: ויולדת טובלות כדרכן בלילי יוה"כ: בעל קרי. שאסור בדברי תורה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi: seminal emission (keri) on Yom Kippur and the immersion requirement.

rashiTranslations.he: כדקיימא לן (ב"ק דף פב.) עזרא תיקן טבילה לבעלי קריין: טובל
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: והולך עד המנחה. אם אירע לו
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: קרי קודם לכן טובל כדי שיוכל
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: להתפלל תפלת המנחה אבל ראה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: קרי מן המנחה ולמעלה אין מותר
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: לטבול אלא ימתין עד שתחשך ויטבול
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: דסבירא להו לרבנן דתפלת נעי לה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: בלילה ולדידהו פוטרת את של ערבית
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: כדרב והא דקתני אינה פוטרת ר'
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: יוסי היא דאמר כל היום מו תר לטבול
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: ואפילו ראה קרי לאחר תפלת המנחה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: טובל ביום כדי להתפלל תפלת נעילה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: אלמא קסבר תפלת נעילה אין זמנה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi explains the opening discussion of this topic on 88a.

rashiTranslations.he: בלילה ולדידיה אינה פוטרת את של
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: ערבית: ה"ג בתוספתא הזב והזבה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: והמצורע והמצורעת ובועל נדה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: וטמא מת טובלין כדרכן ביוה"כ
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: נדה ויולדת טובלות כדרכן בלילי יום
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: הכפורים בעל קרי טובל והולך כל
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: היום כולו ר' יוסי אומר מן המנחה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: ולמעלה אין יכול לטבול. קשיא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: דרבי יוסי אדרבי יוסי ורבנן אדרבנן:
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: לא קשיא. הא דאמר ר' יוסי הכא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: מן המנחה ולמעלה אין יכול לטבול:
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: בדצלי תפלת נעילה. ביום קודם
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: שראה קרי והא דאמרינן לעיל כל
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: היום בדלא צלי תפלת נעילה כשראה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: הקרי: אי דצלי מאי טעמא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: דרבנן. דמתניתא בתרייתא דאמרי
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: כל היום: קא סברי טבילה בזמנה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi elaborates on the halachic details of this sugya.

rashiTranslations.he: מצוה. וזמנה ביום הוא דכתיב
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: (דברים כג) והיה לפנות ערב ירחץ
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: הלכך דחיא רחיצה דיום הכפורים
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: ולאו היינו הך רבנן דלעיל ותרתי
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: פלוגתא נינהו במתני' קמייתא פליגי
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: בזמן תפלת נעילה דלרבנן בלילה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: ולר' יוסי ביום ורבנן דהתם
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: סבירא להו טבילה בזמנה לאו מצוה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: דתדחה יום הכפורים ובמתניתא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: בתרייתא פליגי בטבילה בזמנה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: מצוה: לא ירחוץ. שלא ימחוק את
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: השם: כורך עליו גמי. להגין עליו
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: משטף המים: וקיימא לן. במסכת
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: שבת דבטבילה בזמנה מצוה פליגי
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: דלרבנן טבילה בזמנה לאו מצוה ואם אין לו גמי ימתין עד למחר
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: ויבקשנו: ההיא. דאמרי' לעיל ר' יוסי אומר מן המנחה ולמעלה
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: לא יטבול עד שתחשך דטבילה בזמנה לאו מצוה ר' יוסי בר
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi clarifies the ruling and its application.

rashiTranslations.he: יהודה היא: דיה לטבילה שתהא באחרונה. ברייתא היא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi notes this is R. Yehudah's opinion: 'it is sufficient for the immersion to be the last [act].'

rashiTranslations.he: דמייתינן לה במסכת נדה בגמרא דהמפלת (דף כט:) האשה
rashiTranslations.en: This is a baraita brought in tractate Niddah (29b) in the chapter of ha-Mapelet: a woman who went out [of her husband's house] full [pregnant] and returned empty [having miscarried],

rashiTranslations.he: שיצתה מלאה ובאת ריקנית והביאה לפנינו שלשה שבועים
rashiTranslations.en: ...and brought before us [records of] three clean weeks and ten weeks with alternating impure and pure,

rashiTranslations.he: עהורים ועשרה שבועים אחד טמא ואחד טהור משמשת אור
rashiTranslations.en: ...she immerses on the evening of the thirty-fifth day, and they count ninety-five immersions for her - according to Bet Shammai.

rashiTranslations.he: לשלשים וחמש ומטבילין אותה תשעים וחמש טבילות דברי ב"ש
rashiTranslations.en: Bet Hillel says thirty-five. The Talmud explains all these cases there. The reasoning is that immersion at its proper time is a mitzvah.

rashiTranslations.he: וב"ה אומרים שלשים וחמש וכולהו מפרש להו התם וטעמא משום
rashiTranslations.en: R. Yose bar Yehudah says: it is sufficient for the immersion to be last [rather than at each precise time] - for immersion at its exact time is not a mitzvah.

rashiTranslations.he: טבילה בזמנה מצוה רבי יוסי בר יהודה אומר דיה לטבילה
rashiTranslations.en: And R. Yose referred to here is R. Yose ben Halafta.

rashiTranslations.he: שתהא באחרונה דטבילה בזמנה לאו מצוה ור' יוסי דגמי הוא ר'
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi on 'and in the evening he shall wash' (ve-larev yishafshef): [the concern is] chatzitzah (interposition). 'In the evening, you think so?'...

rashiTranslations.he: יוסי בן חלפתא: ולערב ישפשף.  משום חציצה: לערב ס"ד.
rashiTranslations.en: Once he immersed and emerged, what benefit does washing do? If there was an interposition, it already happened.

rashiTranslations.he: מכי טביל וסליק מאי מהני ליה שפשוף חציצה דהואי הואי:
rashiTranslations.en: Read it instead as 'from the previous evening' - meaning every person should wash himself with hot water [the night before], so that if he sees keri the next day, he can immerse without any interposition.

rashiTranslations.he: אימא מבערב. דאתמול ישפשף כל אדם עצמו בחמין שאם יראה
rashiTranslations.en: And in the evening - meaning from the prior evening - he should wash. Because if he sees...

rashiTranslations.he: קרי למחר יכול לטבול בלא חציצה: הרואה קרי ביוה"כ. ולא
rashiTranslations.en: ...keri the next day he can immerse without interposition. Rashi on 'one who sees keri on Yom Kippur' - without intention.

rashiTranslations.he: במתכוין: עונותיו מחולין. סימן טוב הוא זה יראה זרע ויאריך ימים: ידאג כל השנה כולה. שמא לא קיבלו תעניתו והשביעוהו
rashiTranslations.en: 'His sins are forgiven' - it is a good sign: 'he shall see seed and live long' (Isaiah 53). He should worry all year...

rashiTranslations.he: במה שבידם להשביעו כעבד המוזג כום לרבו ושפך לו קיתון על פגיו: ואם עלתה לו שנה. שלא מת מובטח לו שמעשים
rashiTranslations.en: ...for perhaps his fast was not accepted and [the heavenly court] satiated him with what was in their power, like a servant who pours a cup for his master and then dumps a pitcher of water on his face.

rashiTranslations.he: טובים יש בידו שהגינו עליו ובן עולם הבא הוא: תדע שהרי כל העולם רעב. מתשמיש והוא שבע ולא נתענה בזאת ושלא
rashiTranslations.en: 'And if his year goes well for him' - that he did not die - it is guaranteed that he has good deeds which protected him and he is a person of the World to Come.

rashiTranslations.he: מדעתו השביעוהו ואעפ"כ עלתה לו שנה יש לדעת שצדיק גמור הוא: מפיש חיי. זה שראה קרי ביום הכפורים: סגי ומסגי.
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi on 'prolongs life' (mafis chayyei): this refers to the one who saw keri on Yom Kippur.

rashiTranslations.he: בבנים ובני בנים שכך הסימן יראה זרע יאריך ימים [ישעיה נג]:
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi on 'suffices and causes others to suffice' (sagi u-masgi): in sons and grandchildren, for thus is the sign - 'he shall see seed and live long' (Isaiah 53).

rashiTranslations.he: הדרן עלך יום הכפרים וסליקא לה מסכת יומא
rashiTranslations.en: Rashi notes the Hadran closing formula: 'We shall return to you, Yom ha-Kippurim, and Tractate Yoma is complete.'
